description_long: This object consists of three upright grave markers, each mounted on a rectangular stone base. The markers are arranged side by side, each with distinct coloration and surface detailing: one is a weathered light stone with subtle carvings, the middle is a clean white stone with simple relief patterns, and the third is a dark stone with intricate, high-contrast engravings. The bases are textured to resemble rough, natural stone, and each marker rises vertically with a slightly irregular, aged appearance. The engravings and patterns on the markers suggest cultural or historical significance, with the dark marker featuring the most elaborate motifs. The overall structure is solid and heavy, typical of traditional graveyard monuments, and is designed to be a permanent fixture in an outdoor setting.
description: A trio of upright stone grave markers, each with unique carvings and colors, mounted on rough stone bases and arranged side by side.
description_view_0: Side view showing the narrow profiles of the three grave markers, highlighting their thickness and the layered arrangement.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the detailed engravings and distinct colors of each grave marker, with all three bases visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view emphasizing the backs of the markers and the texture of the stone bases.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the plain backs of the markers and the weathered surfaces of the stone bases.
